steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. The text opens with the narrator's general observation about boat crews, then illustrates it with specific examples involving Harris and George. The correct answer must capture both that opening generalization and the supporting examples.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To give an overview of a particular situation that the narrator finds startling.’: The narrator says he has “always noticed” this—it is a recurring, familiar pattern, not something surprising. “Startling” directly contradicts the tone. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To examine how the narrator and his friends each contributed to navigating a challenge.’: The text does not describe a challenge being navigated or how labor was actually divided. It describes each person’s mistaken belief that he alone did all the work—no shared problem-solving is shown. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To present the narrator’s generalization along with supporting examples from a specific situation.’: The first sentence is an explicit generalization (“I have always noticed that it is the fixed idea of each member of the crew…”). Harris’s and George’s contrasting views then serve as the specific examples. Structure matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘To convey the narrator’s confidence that he understands the role expected of him in a group.’: The narrator describes the overconfidence of Harris and George, not his own sense of his role. Nothing in the passage establishes what the narrator believes about himself. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
